agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering expedited courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and a conditional refund policy for missed delivery windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery tier targeting a 2-hour delivery window from order placement to delivery completion, available to eligible customers within range of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A fixed surcharge ($12.99) applied on top of the base delivery fee for express-tier service." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge applied to all orders regardless of tier. Not subject to express refund policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe starting from order placement within which the express delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time between order placement and physical pickup by the assigned driver. Exceeding this jeopardizes the delivery window." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Customer", "definition": "A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse, making them eligible to select express delivery." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a minimum 4-star rating who is eligible to fulfill express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "When an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour target timeframe from order placement." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A refund of the express fee (not the base fee) issued as a customer goodwill gesture — not a contractual obligation — when a delivery window is missed through no fault of the customer." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute maximum time allowed to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation, after which an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Fault", "definition": "A condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, disqualifying them from receiving a goodwill refund for a missed window." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — logistics cannot fulfill a 2-hour target if the origin-to-destination distance exceeds operational range. Allowing orders outside this radius corrupts the service promise.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer with a delivery address >10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option must not be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by finance or product leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order, the total delivery charge equals base_delivery_fee + $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint — the 2-hour delivery window is physically unachievable if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a hard prerequisite for service integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order placed at time T, a driver must arrive at the warehouse for pickup by T+15 minutes or the order is escalated."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or higher may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality threshold that operations leadership could adjust based on driver pool availability. A VP could lower it to 3.5 in a driver shortage.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, they must never appear in the express-eligible driver pool."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an express delivery window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "The base delivery service was still rendered. Refunding it would mean paying for a completed service, which is a financial integrity violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express delivery window triggering a refund, the refund amount must equal exactly the express fee, not the base fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued for a missed express window if the customer provided a wrong address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Fault-based exclusion — the customer caused the failure. Refunding despite customer fault corrupts the incentive model and creates a fraud vector.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window where the delivery was to a wrong address provided by the customer, refund amount must be $0."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Goodwill refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is an operational SLA that finance could accelerate or extend based on capacity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an approved express refund, the refund must be initiated within 3 business days of approval."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If replacement is not assigned within 10 minutes, an automatic refund of the express fee is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute threshold is an operational policy. The auto-refund is a customer success policy, not a physical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an active express delivery, if no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, a refund of the express fee must be automatically initiated."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express deliveries must provide real-time driver tracking to the customer.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time visibility is a core differentiator of the express product. Without it, the premium positioning has no justification — the customer is paying extra with no transparency.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an active express delivery, the customer must be able to see the driver's location updating in real time (within 30 seconds of actual position)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is the 10km boundary inclusive or exclusive? Must be defined. Recommend inclusive (<=10km)." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "The delivery should be completed by the current driver. Rating eligibility is checked at assignment time, not during delivery." },
    { "scenario": "No express-qualified drivers available within 15-minute pickup window", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the order be auto-downgraded to standard with express fee refunded, or should placement be rejected upfront? Critical gap." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides wrong address AND driver cancels mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — wrong-address exclusion conflicts with the auto-refund on failed replacement. Which rule takes priority?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver is assigned at minute 9, but then also cancels", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 10-minute clock reset per cancellation or is it cumulative from first cancellation?" },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:55 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight / business day boundary", "expected_behavior": "Delivery window is 2 clock hours regardless of day boundaries. Refund processing 'business days' start from the next business day if missed window occurs on a weekend." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse goes offline or runs out of stock after express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — no stakeholder addressed supply-side failures. Must define cancellation and refund behavior for warehouse-caused failures." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does this count as 'wrong address'? Does the 2-hour window reset? Is new address re-validated for 10km eligibility?" },
    { "scenario": "GPS/tracking system goes down during express delivery", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — real-time tracking is a core requirement. No degraded-mode behavior was discussed." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — no warehouse selection logic discussed. Nearest? Most inventory? Shortest estimated delivery?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee at $12.99 creates a new revenue stream but the refund policy (missed windows, auto-refund on failed replacement) creates direct cost exposure. Wrong-address exclusion is a critical fraud prevention measure. The 3-business-day refund timeline affects cash flow. No cap on refund volume was discussed — a systemic delivery failure could trigger mass refunds.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA requires pre-positioned drivers near warehouses. 4-star driver requirement shrinks the eligible driver pool. 10-minute replacement window demands standby driver capacity. Real-time tracking requires continuous GPS infrastructure. All of these create significant operational overhead compared to standard delivery.",
    "user": "Premium customers expect premium reliability. The contradiction between 'guarantee' messaging and 'best effort' legal reality will erode trust if not handled carefully. Real-time tracking is high-value. The refund policy creates a safety net that encourages trial. However, wrong-address exclusion could cause disputes if 'wrong address' is not clearly defined.",
    "risk": "HIGH — The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a reputational and legal liability. If marketing says 'guaranteed' and legal says 'best effort,' customer complaints and potential regulatory scrutiny follow. The auto-refund on replacement failure without a wrong-address check could be exploited. No discussion of force majeure (weather, traffic, infrastructure failure).",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with faster delivery, but confusing guarantee language could set wrong expectations and drive dissatisfaction when windows are missed." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Higher pressure — 15-minute pickup, 4-star minimum creates a two-tier driver class. Express-eligible drivers may bear disproportionate workload. No mention of express driver compensation premium." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup SLA, implying express orders need priority picking and staging. No warehouse-side process was discussed." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue stream offset by refund liability. Need refund rate forecasting and fraud monitoring. 3-business-day processing timeline needs system support." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, missed windows, and the guarantee-vs-best-effort confusion. Need clear escalation policies." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must ensure all customer-facing language avoids 'guarantee' framing. Refund policy must be clearly positioned as goodwill. Terms of service update required." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is low due to a fundamental contradiction between PM/Customer Success ('guaranteed 2-hour delivery') and Legal ('best effort SLA, not a guarantee'). This is not a minor wording issue — it affects the entire product positioning, customer-facing language, refund policy framing, and regulatory exposure. Additionally, multiple critical operational gaps remain unaddressed (no-driver-available scenario, warehouse failures, address change handling, replacement driver cascading failures). The spec is discovery-stage raw notes, not a refined product requirement.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is clearly stated by Finance",
      "10km eligibility radius is unambiguous",
      "15-minute pickup SLA is clearly stated with operational justification",
      "4-star driver minimum is clearly stated",
      "Wrong-address refund exclusion is clearly stated",
      "Refund scope (express fee only, not base fee) is clearly stated"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort — PM and Legal directly contradict",
      "Customer-facing language and positioning of the delivery window",
      "Behavior when no express-qualified driver is available",
      "Warehouse-side fulfillment process and failure modes",
      "Interaction between wrong-address exclusion and auto-refund on replacement failure",
      "Driver compensation model for express tier",
      "Force majeure and systemic failure scenarios"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance — this is unspecified",
      "Driver rating is a rolling average, not per-delivery — rating calculation method unspecified",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup — inferred from Operations Lead's comment but not explicitly confirmed",
      "Business days for refund processing follow standard Mon-Fri excluding public holidays — jurisdiction unspecified",
      "Real-time tracking means sub-30-second location updates — no latency requirement was stated"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' that should be 'shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states it cannot be guaranteed in writing and is a 'best effort SLA' with refunds being a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This is a foundational contradiction that affects product positioning, customer-facing UX copy, refund policy enforceability, and regulatory risk. The entire product identity depends on resolving this.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Position as 'best effort' with goodwill refund — legally safe but weaker product appeal",
        "B: Position as 'guarantee' with contractual refund obligation — strong product appeal but legal exposure",
        "C: Hybrid — use language like 'Express Promise' or '2-Hour Target' that implies commitment without contractual guarantee, with refund positioned as policy not obligation"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — create a branded term (e.g., 'Express Promise') that conveys commitment without legal guarantee. Refund policy framed as company policy, not contractual right. Requires Legal sign-off on exact language before any implementation begins."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when no express-qualified driver is available within the 15-minute pickup window. This is a critical operational scenario — do we reject the order at placement, accept and auto-downgrade, or accept and risk missing the window? Each option has vastly different UX, financial, and operational implications.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Check driver availability before allowing express selection — prevents orphaned express orders",
        "B: Accept all express orders and downgrade with refund if no driver available within 15 min",
        "C: Accept and attempt best-effort without any availability pre-check"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — pre-check driver availability at order time. Accepting an express order with no driver to fulfill it is a guaranteed failure that costs a refund and destroys trust."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The interaction between the wrong-address refund exclusion (BR-6) and the auto-refund on replacement failure (BR-8) is undefined. If a customer gives a wrong address AND the driver cancels AND no replacement is found in 10 minutes, which rule wins? Customer Success says auto-refund; Finance says no refund for wrong address.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Wrong-address exclusion always takes priority — no refund regardless of replacement failure",
        "B: Auto-refund takes priority — system cannot distinguish fault in real-time",
        "C: Flag for manual review — auto-refund is held pending address verification"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — wrong-address is a customer-fault condition that should override all auto-refund triggers. However, this requires a reliable mechanism to determine 'wrong address' in real time, which is itself an unsolved problem."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No discussion of express driver compensation. Drivers are held to a higher standard (4-star minimum) and tighter SLAs (15-minute pickup) but no mention of express delivery pay premium. Without a compensation incentive, high-rated drivers may avoid express assignments, creating a supply problem.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express deliveries pay a flat bonus per delivery",
        "B: Express deliveries pay a percentage premium on base driver rate",
        "C: No premium — rely on existing driver assignment mechanisms"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Escalate to Operations and Finance jointly. Option C will likely fail — the express product depends on driver willingness to accept high-pressure assignments."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
